title: Reposition landing page copy & structure
description:
Rewrite the promo copy/structure in cloud/worker/src/landing.ts for the orchestration-first story while keeping the issue-tracker foundation as a co-equal pillar. Single file = this is the spine of the landing.ts chain. Spec: docs/promo-website-orchestration-update-roadmap.md (sections 1, 1.1, 1.2, 4).

Scope (all within landing.ts):
1. HERO (Candidate A): H1 'The issue tracker your AI agents run on.' Sub: 'Fast, git-native task tracking built for AI agents — that also orchestrates them. Plan a roadmap, run agents in parallel, ship wave by wave.' Keep brand/glow/Catppuccin; only words change.
2. ABOVE-THE-FOLD differentiator strip directly under the hero CTAs (slim chip/row, existing brand tokens — NOT a full section). Carry the user-prioritized hooks:
   - Cost / right-tier: 'Right model for each job — frontier where it pays off, cheaper tiers for the rest. Stop paying premium rates to run one model on everything.'
   - Parallel + managed worktrees: 'Many agents in parallel, each in its own auto-managed git worktree — no babysitting.'
   - Fully git-tracked, no required backend: 'Your issues are JSON in your repo. No daemon, no database, no backend required — cloud sync is optional.'
3. FEATURE GRID -> two labelled pillars (3 cards each) per spec section 4:
   Pillar 1 'A great issue tracker for agents': persistent memory; yours-in-git/no-backend-required; lightning-fast & agent-native.
   Pillar 2 'That orchestrates them': parallel with managed worktrees; right-model-for-each-job; roadmaps/ordering/handoff.
4. HOW IT WORKS: rewrite step 3 around invoking the ticks skill (Claude Code or Codex) + tk graph/tk board, and REPLACE the broken 'tk run epic --board' / 'tk run epic --cloud' lines (the live-broken CTA) with real, working commands only.
5. NAV: point the 'Docs' link to on-site '/docs' (contract with the docs-page tick). 'Read the Docs' CTA -> /docs too.

HONESTY CONSTRAINTS: no specific model names (skill is model-agnostic); cloud sync is opt-in, never imply 'no cloud at all'; every command shown must exist in the cobra command set.

VERIFY: cd cloud/worker && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit (worker file, NOT UI — build-ui.sh does not apply). Visually confirm no 'tk run' remains: grep -n 'tk run' cloud/worker/src/landing.ts returns nothing.
acceptance_criteria: Hero uses Candidate A headline+sub; above-the-fold differentiator strip present (cost/tier, parallel+worktrees, git/no-backend); feature grid shows two labelled pillars (3+3 cards); how-it-works step 3 + code block use ONLY real tk commands (zero 'tk run'); nav Docs -> /docs; cd cloud/worker && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit clean
